EventEntry.description: One entry in the activity feed: a fault, safety-interlock activation, profile application, setpoint edit, or drift detection (frontend data model §4). Severity is the platform's dashboard grading (info/warning/critical) — distinct from the controller's fault severity (warning/alarm).

ts.description: When the event occurred (RFC 3339 UTC, ms).

kind.description: Closed set of event kinds surfaced to the operator. `optimizer_plan_applied` is an applied optimizer plan — a setpoint write stamped `source: optimizer` (RFC-005). `optimizer_plan_escalated` (a cycle held for review), `optimizer_resolved` (an operator closed an escalation), and `optimizer_run_failed` (a cycle that produced no outcome, e.g. `cycle_timeout`/`llm_unavailable`) are the optimizer's escalation-lifecycle + run-failure audit events, stamped `source: optimizer` with the run id and (when applicable) reason code in `message`. These record *transitions* in the append-only feed; the actionable set of currently-open holds stays in the polled optimizer console queue (`GET /api/optimizer/escalations`). `optimizer_plan_extended` is deliberately not a feed kind — a suppressed cycle writes nothing and recurs on most cadences, so it would be feed noise.

severity.description: info = informational; warning = degraded but operating; critical = raises a toast.

source.description: Who or what produced the event (audit attribution, platform §6) — e.g. operator, crop_profile, optimizer.
